---
title: "Chapter 3: Central Tendency"
output: html_document
---

# Central Tendency

To summarize our coffee dataset, we look for a central value. The most common metric is the arithmetic mean.

## The Mean

The mathematical formula for calculating the sample mean $\bar{X}$ is expressed using display math below:

$$\bar{X} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} X_i$$

### Simple Summary Table

Here is a quick look at how the mean compares to the median for our coffee dataset:

| Metric | Formula Type | Typical Value |
|--------|--------------|---------------|
| Mean   | Algebraic    | 3.6 cups      |
| Median | Positional   | 4.0 cups      |

The median provides a robust alternative when datasets contain extreme outliers.
